feedback.text: For me, this regulation is yet another proof of an EU that is often undemocratically seeking to exercise totalitarian control over tax-paying residents. So I stop my opinion as a matter of urgency. Henceforth focus on freedom and limit the EU sec to where it was created: improve the internal market. And according to the idea of the EU and its origins (ECSC, EEC and EC), this is intended to focus on the market at the heart of the word: a “place” where supply and demand meet and where swap transactions are made. No longer abuse the term “subsidiarity” to stretch the term INTERNE MARKT. The EU has not been established to regulate the human behaviour of its residents on the internet. Therefore, ensure clear rules for companies that offer something on the internet (and may abuse it). Look at all the rules from the perspective “is it totalitarian and threatens the sovereignty of the EU?” If the answer to the question is in the affirmative, stop these legislation/rules and start a new one. Governments are looking ahead, in particular looking beyond your own nose and considering how this underlying/proposed regulation has an impact on EU citizens’ freedom and how these rules could potentially be abused by parties (the EU at the forefront)! Be very careful to reduce the number of EU staff in the coming years and make it more effective. Take a look at the EU’s own corruption in Brussels. Reduce the number of lobby contacts between NGOs and businesses with EU policy makers. You are aware that facism means that the government works with businesses and that fascism strives for a totalitarian state to have full control over social life and social and cultural organisations. Look into the mirror and mirror yourself against these aspects of facism. Be honest and transparent. Focus on added value and realise that the countries/residents in Europe are different and have a different cultural background than the US. Ensuring diversity in Europe, be aware of the levelling effect of many EU rules. Respect the different cultures in Europe. And if you go yourself, define clearly in advance what digital fairness is!
